status_notes: Initial research from AWS RDS documentation, 2026-04-04. Needs cross-reference and live environment validation.
notes: Managed relational databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, Aurora). Security includes encryption, network isolation, authentication, automated backups.

match_nodes.description: Encrypt DB instance storage and snapshots with KMS

data.description: Encrypt DB instance and snapshots with KMS. Must be enabled at creation (cannot encrypt existing unencrypted instance). Options: AWS managed key, customer managed key. Default: Enabled for new instances.

options.name: AWS Managed Key
options.description: Default aws/rds KMS key. No additional cost.

options.name: Customer Managed Key
options.description: Customer-managed KMS key. Provides audit trail and key rotation control.

default.note: Encryption at rest is enabled by default for new DB instances.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use a customer managed KMS key for audit trail and key rotation control. Encryption must be enabled at instance creation time.

data.description: TLS/SSL connections to RDS instances. Options: require SSL via parameter group (rds.force_ssl=1 for PostgreSQL, require_secure_transport=ON for MySQL). Each engine has its own parameter.

options.name: Force SSL (PostgreSQL)
options.description: Set rds.force_ssl=1 in parameter group to require SSL for PostgreSQL.

options.name: Require Secure Transport (MySQL)
options.description: Set require_secure_transport=ON in parameter group to require SSL for MySQL.

default.note: SSL/TLS is supported but not required by default. Clients can connect without encryption.

data.vendor_recommendation: Require SSL for all database connections by setting the appropriate parameter group value for your engine.

match_nodes.description: Use IAM credentials instead of database passwords

data.description: Use IAM credentials instead of database passwords. Generates temporary authentication tokens. Supported for MySQL, PostgreSQL, and Aurora. Tokens are valid for 15 minutes.

default.note: IAM database authentication is disabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable IAM authentication to eliminate long-lived database passwords and leverage IAM policies for access control.

audit.console: RDS -> Databases -> {instance} -> Configuration -> IAM DB authentication

match_nodes.description: Control network access to RDS instances via VPC security groups

data.description: Control network access to RDS instances. RDS should be deployed in a private subnet with security groups restricting inbound access to application servers only.
data.vendor_recommendation: Place RDS instances in private subnets. Use security groups to restrict access to application tier only. Never allow 0.0.0.0/0 inbound access.

audit.console: RDS -> Databases -> {instance} -> Connectivity & security -> Security groups

match_nodes.description: Whether RDS instance has a public IP address

data.description: Whether the DB instance has a public IP address. When set to Yes, the instance is assigned a public IP and is reachable from the internet (subject to security group rules).

options.description: Instance has a public IP. Reachable from the internet.
Instance has no public IP. Only reachable from within VPC.

default.note: Default for new instances is No (not publicly accessible).

data.vendor_recommendation: Always set to No. RDS instances should never be publicly accessible in production.

audit.console: RDS -> Databases -> {instance} -> Connectivity & security -> Publicly accessible

data.description: Point-in-time recovery via automated backups. Retention period configurable from 0 to 35 days. Setting to 0 disables automated backups.

default.note: Default backup retention period is 7 days.

data.vendor_recommendation: Set backup retention to at least 7 days. Consider 35 days for production databases with compliance requirements.

audit.console: RDS -> Databases -> {instance} -> Maintenance & backups -> Backup retention period

match_nodes.description: Control who can restore DB snapshots

data.description: Control who can restore DB snapshots. Snapshots can be private (default), shared with specific AWS accounts, or made public (dangerous — exposes data to any AWS account).

options.description: Default. Only the owning account can restore.
Shared with specific AWS account IDs.
Any AWS account can restore. Dangerous — exposes data to anyone.

data.vendor_recommendation: Never make snapshots public. Review shared snapshot permissions regularly.

match_nodes.description: Prevent accidental deletion of RDS instances

data.description: Prevent accidental deletion of DB instances. When enabled, the instance cannot be deleted until deletion protection is explicitly disabled.

default.note: Deletion protection is disabled by default.

data.description: OS-level metrics at configurable intervals. Provides CPU, memory, file system, and disk I/O metrics from the underlying operating system. Requires an IAM role for CloudWatch Logs.

default.note: Enhanced Monitoring is disabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable with at least 60-second granularity for production workloads to aid in troubleshooting and security incident analysis.

data.description: Database performance monitoring that visualizes database load and identifies bottlenecks. Analyzes wait events, SQL queries, and host metrics. Free tier includes 7-day retention.

options.name: Free Tier
options.description: 7-day data retention at no additional cost.

options.name: Long-term Retention
options.description: Up to 2 years of data retention. Paid feature.

default.note: Performance Insights is disabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable Performance Insights for all production instances to aid in query performance analysis and security investigation.
